semanticCore: Shattering, breaking completely, crushing, destroying, or breaking hard things

keyDerivations.meaning: To shatter, break down, crush, annihilate, or break completely

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَكَمْ قَصَمْنَا مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ كَانَتْ ظَالِمَةً وَأَنْشَأْنَا بَعْدَهَا قَوْمًا آخَرِينَ (21:11): And how many a town that was unjust We shattered/crushed, and raised up after it another people.
summaryEn: The root represents crushing or shattering an object (Qasm), applied to the absolute ruin of tyrannical societies whose civilizations were utterly demolished.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الكسر والتهشيم الكلي لشيء صلب والاستئصال والإهلاك المدمر (قصم)، واصفاً تحطيم الله للقرى الظالمة وإزالتها من الوجود.
rawText: قَيْصُومٌ Artemisia abrotanum, or southern-wood; also called قَيْصُومٌ ذَكَرٌ: and قَيْصُومٌ أُنْثَى is applied to santolina.
